practice: Explain the difference between the desktop app and Docker Webswing.
List the ports used by the ZAP Webswing container.
Describe when a headless or containerized setup is preferable.
title: ZAP Lesson 2: Install and Launch
summary: Install ZAP on desktop or run it in Docker with the Webswing UI.
content:
# ZAP Lesson 2: Install and Launch

## Overview
ZAP can run as a desktop application or inside a Docker container. The desktop app requires Java, while Docker images ship with everything you need.

## Desktop setup
- Install the ZAP desktop application
- Ensure the required Java version is installed
- Launch ZAP and open the Quick Start tab

## Docker Webswing
The Webswing container provides the ZAP UI in a browser. It is useful for labs, demos, and shared environments.

## When to choose which
- Desktop: daily interactive testing
- Docker Webswing: quick lab setup, remote access
- Headless CLI: CI pipelines and automation

## Tips
- Use a stable image tag for reproducible results
- Keep scan targets local or authorized
examples: Use Webswing to run ZAP without installing Java locally.
Choose desktop ZAP for a test session that requires manual exploration.
Use a container in CI to keep the environment consistent.
objectives: Describe desktop vs Docker ZAP setups.
Run the Webswing container with the correct ports.
Choose an appropriate deployment option for your workflow.
Identify when headless execution is useful.
